commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=60cfb8b96f4e7b33de6d27c407cca96312a28737)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes routed reply and outbound delivery mirror plumbing so `sourceReplyTranscriptMirror` metadata can reach transcript append, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +45, Tests +138. Total +183 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: PR head passes `mirror: false` when source metadata is absent, while current main mirrors routed replies by default whenever a session key exists. I did not run a live channel reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a narrow code correction plus contributor or maintainer real behavior proof; automation should not take over an external proof-gated setup.

target.title: fix(reply): preserve routed transcript mirror metadata
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves full `sourceReplyTranscriptMirror` metadata when final replies are routed through `routeReply`, instead of collapsing that mirror state down to the minimal outbound shape.
- Extends outbound transcript mirroring so `expectedSessionId`, `storePath`, and `deliveryMirror` survive durable delivery and reach transcript append.
- Adds regression coverage across the routed reply caller path, the `routeReply` boundary, and the outbound transcript append boundary so future reply-routing changes do not silently drop transcript binding metadata.
- Fixes a correctness gap in the routed reply path without changing config, migration behavior, or the user-facing reply model.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

Describe the intent and outcome in 2-5 bullets. Avoid restating the diff; reviewers and bots can read the changed files.

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #92489

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  - Routed final replies were dropping source-reply transcript mirror metadata before transcript append, so `expectedSessionId`, `storePath`, or `deliveryMirror` could be lost across outbound delivery.

- Real environment tested:
  - Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/route-reply.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - `dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
    ```text
     RUN  v4.1.8 /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw

     Test Files  1 passed (1)
          Tests  211 passed (211)
       Start at  23:52:10
       Duration  30.36s (transform 1.98s, setup 108ms, import 588ms, tests 29.59s, environment 0ms)

    [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
    [vitest] still running with no output for 30000ms (test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts).
    [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 33.43s
    ```

  - `route-reply.test.ts`
    ```text
     RUN  v4.1.8 /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw

     Test Files  1 passed (1)
          Tests  40 passed (40)
       Start at  23:52:46
       Duration  543ms (transform 247ms, setup 75ms, import 373ms, tests 36ms, environment 0ms)

    [test] queued behind the local heavy-check lock...
    [test] still waiting 15s for the local heavy-check lock held by test, pid 43489, cwd /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw...
    [test] still waiting 30s for the local heavy-check lock held by test, pid 43489, cwd /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw...
    [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts
    [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 39.96s
    ```

  - `deliver.test.ts`
    ```text
     RUN  v4.1.8 /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw

     Test Files  1 passed (1)
          Tests  97 passed (97)
       Start at  23:52:42
       Duration  2.73s (transform 1.96s, setup 219ms, import 2.03s, tests 396ms, environment 0ms)

    [test] queued behind the local heavy-check lock held by test, pid 43489, cwd /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw...
    [test] still waiting 15s for the local heavy-check lock held by test, pid 43489, cwd /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw...
    [test] still waiting 30s for the local heavy-check lock held by test, pid 43489, cwd /Users/shen/Documents/GitHub/openclaw...
    [test] starting test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
    [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 37.58s
    ```

  - `autoreview --mode local`
    ```text
    autoreview target: local
    branch: fix/issue-94157-manifest-base-path-v2
    engine: codex
    tools: on
    web_search: on
    bundle: 64810 chars
    autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
    overall: patch is correct (0.89)
    The change consistently threads richer transcript-mirror metadata through routed outbound delivery, and the touched call sites line up with the existing transcript append contract. I did not find a concrete regression, security issue, or missing pre-merge fix in the reviewed diff.
    ```

- Observed result after fix:
  - Routed reply coverage now proves `sourceReplyTranscriptMirror` is forwarded through `routeReply`.
  - Outbound delivery coverage now proves transcript append receives `expectedSessionId`, `storePath`, and `deliveryMirror`.
  - Local autoreview completed clean after the final patch.

- What was not tested:
  - No live Slack/Telegram end-to-end run against a real external account.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  - I validated the fix with targeted regression coverage and autoreview in a local source checkout, but I did not have a live external channel setup available in this environment.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  - Before this patch, the routed path reduced transcript mirror state to the minimal outbound mirror shape, so richer source-reply metadata was dropped before transcript append.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/route-reply.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added routed final reply coverage in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` to assert that `sourceReplyTranscriptMirror` is forwarded through `routeReply`.
- Added explicit mirror metadata coverage in `src/auto-reply/reply/route-reply.test.ts` to assert that outbound delivery receives the richer mirror object.
- Added outbound transcript append coverage in `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts` to assert that `expectedSessionId`, `storePath`, and `deliveryMirror` survive durable delivery.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The routed reply path only preserved the minimal outbound mirror fields and dropped richer source-reply transcript metadata before transcript append.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No

What is the highest-risk area?

- Routed reply delivery and outbound transcript mirroring

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change is scoped to metadata preservation, not new routing behavior.
- Regression tests cover the caller path, the `routeReply` boundary, and the outbound transcript append boundary.
- Local autoreview completed clean after the final patch.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer review
- CI
- Optional maintainer-requested live channel proof if source-level regression coverage is not enough for this path

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed local autoreview finding that routed transcript mirror metadata was still being dropped before outbound transcript append.
- Addressed local autoreview finding about an incorrect type import in the first patch iteration.

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>
